title: QUIC RFC 9000 CRYPTO Retransmission ACK Piggyback Work Item
summary: Implement and prove fresh pending ACK prefixing for rebuilt Initial and Handshake CRYPTO retransmission packets without treating old ACK bytes as retransmittable data.
planned_changes:
- Extend the long-header CRYPTO retransmission packet builders with a transient prefix-frame payload used for fresh ACK frame bytes.
- Wire the runtime CRYPTO retransmission rebuild path to prepend pending ACK frames for the matching Initial or Handshake packet number space.
- Mark piggybacked retransmission ACK frames as sent only after the rebuilt packet is tracked under its actual new packet number.
- Preserve existing behavior when no ACK is pending.
- Keep retry bootstrap replay, retried Initial replay, and broader delayed/path/interop scheduling outside this slice.
- Add focused positive, negative, deterministic fuzz/property, and BenchmarkDotNet coverage for the retransmission packet-builder path.
verification_plan: Run the focused RFC 9000 S13P2P1-0010 requirement-home tests, run the adjacent RFC 9002 CRYPTO retransmission/PTO tests, run the long-header packet-protection benchmark suite with BenchmarkDotNet Dry and Short jobs, render/check the touched SpecTrace artifacts, validate JSON and preserve standing failures if present, run `git diff --check`, and preserve evidence under `artifacts/verification/20260425-rfc9000-crypto-retransmission-ack-piggyback/` and `artifacts/benchmark-baseline/20260425-rfc9000-crypto-retransmission-ack-piggyback/`.
out_of_scope: Application Data stream and NEW_TOKEN ACK piggybacking already owned by earlier slices.
completion_notes: Completed as a bounded Initial and Handshake CRYPTO retransmission packet-composition floor. Broader retry/replay, delayed-ACK scheduling, path scheduling, interop, and public API behavior remain outside this work item.
